title: Multi-thread depth on enterprise deals doubled since Q1
anchor: Active opportunities above $250K ACV now average 4.2 engaged contacts versus 2.1 in Q1.
connect: Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% compared to 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
body: Active opportunities above $250K ACV now average 4.2 engaged contacts versus 2.1 in Q1. Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% compared to 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
trace: Enterprise multi-threading improvement correlates with improved win rates across contact depth bands.

title: Sterling buying committee expanded after executive demo
anchor: The April 18 executive demo added CFO, business-unit lead, and second business-unit contact, growing the committee from 4 to 7.
connect: Committees that grow during evaluation close at 26% above flat committees historically.
body: The April 18 executive demo added CFO, business-unit lead, and second business-unit contact, growing the committee from 4 to 7. Committees that grow during evaluation close at 26% above flat committees historically.
trace: Sterling deal committee expansion during evaluation phase signals positive buying momentum.

title: Single-threaded coverage dropped 12 points in Q2
anchor: Single-threaded deals now represent 22% of open pipeline versus 34% in Q1.
connect: Q3 pipeline with live engagement in the last 14 days sits at $3.3M of the $4.2M total.
body: Single-threaded deals now represent 22% of open pipeline versus 34% in Q1. Q3 pipeline with live engagement in the last 14 days sits at $3.3M of the $4.2M total.
trace: Multi-threading discipline improvement corresponds with higher active engagement rates across the pipe.

title: Champion documentation crosses 50% threshold in Q2
anchor: 51% of active opportunities carry a named Champion field in CRM, the first quarter above 50%.
connect: Deals with Champion documentation win at 38% versus 21% without.
body: 51% of active opportunities carry a named Champion field in CRM, the first quarter above 50%. Deals with Champion documentation win at 38% versus 21% without.
trace: Champion field hygiene milestone corresponds with significant win rate divergence.

title: Mid-market win rate climbs 8 points quarter over quarter
anchor: Q2 mid-market closes at 27.1% versus Q1's 19.1%, with 19 wins from 70 opportunities.
connect: ICP-aligned deals account for 89% of the Q2 win rate improvement.
body: Q2 mid-market closes at 27.1% versus Q1's 19.1%, with 19 wins from 70 opportunities. ICP-aligned deals account for 89% of the Q2 win rate improvement.
trace: Mid-market segment win rate acceleration driven primarily by ICP discipline rather than broad execution improvement.

title: Beacon head-to-head win rate jumps to 61%
anchor: Q2 Beacon competitive deals close at 11 wins from 18 opportunities versus trailing four quarters at 24%.
connect: Beacon appears in 29% of competitive opportunities this quarter, up from 19% in Q1.
body: Q2 Beacon competitive deals close at 11 wins from 18 opportunities versus trailing four quarters at 24%. Beacon appears in 29% of competitive opportunities this quarter, up from 19% in Q1.
trace: Beacon competitive performance reversal coincides with increased competitive frequency.

title: Beacon battlecard utilization more than doubles
anchor: 72% of active Beacon head-to-head deals use the battlecard in April versus 31% in February.
connect: Head-to-head win rate on Beacon deals climbed 14 points over the same window.
body: 72% of active Beacon head-to-head deals use the battlecard in April versus 31% in February. Head-to-head win rate on Beacon deals climbed 14 points over the same window.
trace: Battlecard adoption surge corresponds directly with competitive win rate improvement against Beacon.

title: Meridian AI appears in 26% of Q2 competitive deals
anchor: 12 of 47 active deals feature Meridian as a competitor, up from 10% in Q1.
connect: 7 of 12 Meridian deals reached negotiation within 30 days of their $25M Series B announcement March 14.
body: 12 of 47 active deals feature Meridian as a competitor, up from 10% in Q1. 7 of 12 Meridian deals reached negotiation within 30 days of their $25M Series B announcement March 14.
trace: Meridian competitive presence increases alongside funding announcement momentum in active deals.

title: Enterprise proposal cycle compresses 11 days
anchor: Proposal-to-Closed-Won median sits at 38 days in Q2 versus 49 days in Q1.
connect: Q2 enterprise closes with multi-thread depth 5+ account for 7 of 9 total enterprise wins.
body: Proposal-to-Closed-Won median sits at 38 days in Q2 versus 49 days in Q1. Q2 enterprise closes with multi-thread depth 5+ account for 7 of 9 total enterprise wins.
trace: Enterprise proposal acceleration correlates with deep multi-threading in closed deals.

title: Aged proposal deals advance after re-issue
anchor: 14 proposal-stage deals aged beyond 21 days as of April, with 11 advancing or closing in the last 30 days.
connect: 6 of the 11 advances came within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
body: 14 proposal-stage deals aged beyond 21 days as of April, with 11 advancing or closing in the last 30 days. 6 of the 11 advances came within 5 days of a re-issued proposal.
trace: Proposal aging pattern shows re-issue timing as a critical momentum factor for stalled deals.

title: Champion re-engagement drives stage advancement
anchor: 9 of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement in April.
connect: Champion contact lapses over 7 days triggered re-engagement events April 14 and March 6.
body: 9 of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement in April. Champion contact lapses over 7 days triggered re-engagement events April 14 and March 6.
trace: Champion re-engagement timing shows strong correlation with subsequent deal progression.

title: CRM activity capture reaches 88% completeness
anchor: 23 of 26 closed Q2 deals carry full activity history, outcome reason, and stakeholder mapping versus Q1's 64%.
connect: 19 of the 23 complete captures landed after the April 5 outcome reason field launch.
body: 23 of 26 closed Q2 deals carry full activity history, outcome reason, and stakeholder mapping versus Q1's 64%. 19 of the 23 complete captures landed after the April 5 outcome reason field launch.
trace: CRM hygiene improvement accelerated by outcome reason field implementation in early April.

title: Revised outbound sequence hits 14% conversion
anchor: 42 of 300 outbound contacts booked discovery calls since the April 5 cadence launch.
connect: Prior outbound sequence ran at 9% sequence-to-meeting conversion.
body: 42 of 300 outbound contacts booked discovery calls since the April 5 cadence launch. Prior outbound sequence ran at 9% sequence-to-meeting conversion.
trace: Outbound sequence revision delivers 56% improvement in contact-to-meeting conversion rate.

title: Inbound conversion holds at 31% versus revised outbound
